segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They cut off the road at noon to skirt a flooded bend and followed a sheep path along a hedgerow prickled with last year's hawthorn. Water ran high in the ditch -- brown and cold, tea-bitter with tannin. The air smelled of wet wool, iron, and green things just remembering how to live.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex stopped so abruptly Caelin nearly walked into her. One gloved hand angled down: halt.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She'd seen it: a drag across the ditch slime where something heavy had been pulled, reeds crushed in a neat human pattern. On the hedge's far side, the faintest glint of brass where no brass should be.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the hedge ahead, a voice with too much bravado and not enough patience:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three men broke the hedge at once -- two with crossbows, one with a short fowling spear. They wore leather jacks with mismatched studs and the expressions of people who hadn't been paid yet.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said to Caelin without looking.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the spear carrier said, eyes on Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind Caelin, something thinned in the light. Vex didn't turn, but she felt it: Durgan's shadow flattening like a hound belly-crawling before a pounce.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, keeping his voice level.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One of the men smiled. It was not a good smile.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex moved. Not the dramatic leap of a stage knife but a hunter's step -- heel-to-ball, weight forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A flick of wrist and the spearman's weapon wasn't in his hand anymore; it was in the ditch with a splash, and he was bleeding from the web of skin between thumb and forefinger in a way that made him as angry as it did clumsy.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Heat spiked up the lattice in his arm -- fast, focused, controlled. He punched his palm at the ground between them, not at the men. Air went hard in a fan and the ditchwater hissed -- stone to glass in a strip three feet wide.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bolts hit the newborn slick and skated sideways into the hedge, thunk-thunk, harmless.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pain arrived half a beat later. Not rupturing. Not yet. A white wire drawn through the filaments, singing in his teeth. Nyxara's lesson -- heat on one side, cold on the other, the border doing the work -- had turned a blast into a deflection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three out of ten, she'd said. Maybe three and a half today.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They didn't. The second crossbowman yelped something about

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: and crabbed sideways to get a shot. Durgan's blade touched his throat from behind before he finished the motion.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said. Calm. Too calm. The calm of a man holding something back with both hands and only one of them visible.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The man dropped it. The bowstring twanged and sang a sad little note as it hit the glass strip and spun.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The spear carrier dove for his weapon in the ditch, came up with cold mud and a broken glove. Vex met him halfway -- rib to elbow, knee to thigh, heel to instep -- a conversation he lost quickly and with regret.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She retrieved the dropped spear and snapped it over her knee just because it felt good. The rib reminded her immediately that it had opinions about that. She absorbed the flash of pain without showing it, mostly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan released the man he'd collared -- gently, considering -- and stepped back like he'd touched a stove. His nose was bleeding. He wiped it onto black cloth that wouldn't show it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin exhaled, shakier than he wanted to be. The filaments along his forearm thrummed angrily then settled to their baseline ache -- manageable. For now.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Easier to slide on than stand on."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Going to assume you can only do that a handful of times before you chew your arm off."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She nodded, cataloguing his limits the way she catalogued everything.
